claims.claim: CBS settlement (semel yishuv) codes: Jerusalem 3000, Tel Aviv-Yafo 5000, Haifa 4000, Rishon LeZion 8300, Petah Tikva 7900, Ashdod 70, Netanya 7400, Beer Sheva 9000, Ramat Gan 8600, Modiin-Maccabim-Reut 1200, Nazareth 7300, Eilat 2600. Israel has roughly 1,270 localities.
The Israeli postal code (mikud) is 7 digits, introduced 1 February 2013 (replacing the older 5-digit code).

Israel Post's mikud lookup is a web form at doar.israelpost.co.il/locatezip; there is no official public mikud-by-address API. The bennymeg/IsraelPostalServiceAPI library is a shipping-price calculator, not a mikud lookup.
GovMap provides an official programmatic geocoding / address-search API at api.govmap.gov.il (email registration required); the national map UI is at govmap.gov.il.

The Survey of Israel block/parcel-by-address (gush/helka) tool is at gov.il/apps/mapi/parcel_address/parcel_address.html; the old mapi.gov.il/Pages/LotAddressLocator.aspx path was retired and now redirects to a generic landing page.
data.gov.il hosts the street (רחובות) and locality (ישובים) datasets, queryable via the CKAN datastore_search API; fields include city_code / city_name_he / city_name_en.
The Israel Central Bureau of Statistics (CBS / Lamas) at cbs.gov.il publishes the official settlement and locality codes.
Illustrative example: Rothschild 42, Tel Aviv-Yafo, mikud 6688312, CBS code 5000. Example block/parcel 6213/250.
